title: New Vampire Movie Box Office Opening Continues Disappointing Horror Trend
text: The vampire horror-comedy Abigail has underperformed at the box office, earning $10 million domestically and $15.2 million globally during its opening weekend. The film stars Melissa Barrera and Dan Stevens. Despite receiving strong critical reviews with an 83% Rotten Tomatoes score, the movie continues a broader pattern affecting 2024 horror releases. As the article notes, major 2024 horror movies have had a similarly lukewarm commercial reception. The film's $28 million budget means it needs approximately $56 million worldwide to break even, making its current trajectory concerning. Other recent horror releases show comparable struggles: Night Swim ($15M budget): $54.1M worldwide. The First Omen ($30M budget): $45M worldwide. Lisa Frankenstein ($13M budget): $9.9M worldwide. The industry has reasons for optimism. Upcoming releases include franchise films like A Quiet Place: Day One, Alien: Romulus, Smile 2, and Terrifier 3, plus smaller titles generating buzz such as Longlegs and The Watchers. These projects could reverse the genre's current box office struggles before year's end.
